message:
fix(core): CRDT tombstones, cancellation deadlock, cooldown panic, entropy decay, UTF-8 dead code

Five correctness fixes in frankenterm-core:

1. crdt.rs — Fix OR-Set remove semantics with tombstones:
   The OrSet (Observed-Remove Set) CRDT had a fundamental bug: remove()
   simply deleted entries from the map, so when two replicas merged, a
   removed element would reappear because the other replica still held
   the tag. Introduces a tombstones BTreeSet to properly track removed
   tags. remove() now moves tags to tombstones; contains(), len(), and
   elements() filter out tombstoned tags; merge() unions tombstones from
   both sides. Uses #[serde(default)] for backward compatibility.

2. cancellation.rs — Fix recursive lock deadlock:
   propagate_to_children() and propagate_inner() held the mutex lock
   while recursively propagating to children, which could deadlock if a
   child's propagation tried to acquire the same lock. Now clones the
   children vec and drops the lock before iterating. Also deduplicates
   inline propagation logic by delegating to propagate_inner().

3. cooldown_tracker.rs — Fix Instant::duration_since panic:
   Two calls to now.duration_since(self.last_seen) replaced with
   now.saturating_duration_since(self.last_seen) in is_expired() and
   remaining(). duration_since panics if now < last_seen (possible with
   clock adjustments); saturating_duration_since returns Duration::ZERO.

4. aegis_entropy_anomaly.rs — Fix E-value decay direction:
   The decay logic (self.e_value *= self.decay) drove E-value toward 0,
   but E-value represents a statistical measure where 1.0 is baseline.
   Replaced with bidirectional decay: if above 1.0, decay the excess;
   if below 1.0, decay the deficit. Both now converge to 1.0.

5. utf8_chunked.rs — Remove dead code:
   Removed trailing_incomplete_len() (always returned 0) and simplified
   the Ok(_) branch of from_utf8 match by removing 20 lines of dead
   logic that checked for trailing incomplete sequences after from_utf8
   succeeded (impossible — from_utf8 only succeeds on complete UTF-8).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
